commit.message:
Cache version-resilient hash code on MethodTableAuxiliaryData to fix superlinear behavior with polymorphic recursion (#126534)

`GetVersionResilientTypeHashCode` recomputes hash codes recursively
without memoization. With polymorphic recursion, each level doubles the
number of unique generic instantiations, causing exponential blowup —
`n=31` took ~15s before this fix.

On 64-bit release builds, `MethodTableAuxiliaryData` already had 4 bytes
of implicit alignment padding between the union and `m_pLoaderModule`.

---------

commit.message:
feat(frontend): add tabbed UI and Playwright e2e tests (#20)

## Summary

Fixes the blank frontend (Svelte 5 mount API) and refactors the UI with
tabs. Adds Playwright e2e test suite covering all GUI requirements.

## Changes

### Bug fix
- Replace `new App()` (Svelte 4) with `mount()` (Svelte 5) in `main.ts`
— this was causing the blank page

### Frontend refactor
- Single card with **Create / Edit / Delete / Test redirect** tabs —
only active tab is visible
- Improved `style.css`: tab bar, danger-styled Delete button, result-row
layout

### Playwright e2e tests (26 tests, all passing)
| File | Coverage |
|---|---|
| `navigation.spec.ts` | Tab switching, mutual exclusivity |
| `create-link.spec.ts` | Fields, redirect codes 301/302/307/308,
success + 400/409 errors |
| `edit-link.spec.ts` | Partial update options, token validation,
401/409 errors |
| `delete-link.spec.ts` | Danger styling, success + 401/404 errors |
| `test-redirect.spec.ts` | Active/inactive links, 404 unknown ID |

All tests mock the API via `page.route()` so they run without a live
backend.

## Running tests
```bash
bash run-ui-tests.sh
```
Requires the frontend running at `http://localhost:5173` (`docker
compose up` or `npm run dev`).

commit.message:
hew-serialize: extract shared SideTableVisitor walker for side-table builders (#889)

Replace three near-identical recursive AST walkers with a single generic
walk_program<V: SideTableVisitor> function. The three build_*_entries
public functions now each supply a small local Visitor impl instead of
carrying a full copy of the traversal skeleton (~350 lines each).

Key design decisions:
- IfStmtOrder enum preserves the existing behavioral difference between
  the assign-kind/method-receiver walkers (ElseBeforeThen) and the
  assign-shape walker (ThenBeforeElse) in Stmt::If traversal.
- ModuleGraphMode enum preserves the two distinct module-graph traversal
  policies: ItemsThenNonRootModules (assign walkers) vs
  ModulesOrProgramItems (method-receiver walker).
- on_assign_stmt hook covers the Stmt::Assign entry-extraction point;
  on_method_call_expr covers the Expr::MethodCall entry-extraction point.
- No schema changes, no public API changes, no behavioral changes.

Net: -512 lines, 130/130 tests pass.

commit.message:
feat(research): EXP-1571 robustness archetype characterization

Classifies 11 patients into robustness tiers based on how their
meal-clock regularity responds to detection parameter changes:
- Robust (45%): b, c, f, g, j — σσ < 0.6
- Moderate (18%): h, i — σσ 0.6–1.0
- Sensitive (36%): a, d, e, k — σσ ≥ 1.0

Key finding: n_peaks is the strongest predictor of robustness
(ρ = −0.851, p = 0.0009). 3+ meal peaks = parameter-resilient.

Includes fig34-36 (archetype distribution, stability curves,
tier profiles) and Phase 8 report.

commit.message:
Fix: Support both old and new metadata schemas in step3

The step2_download_era5_parallel.py script was recently updated to use 'first_time'
and 'last_time' columns instead of 'start_time' and 'end_time' (commit 5f9f8f3).

However, step3_precompute_composites.py was still hardcoded to expect 'start_time',
causing KeyError: 'start_time' when processing data files created with the new
step2 schema while trying to get the case month for climatology lookups.

This fix:
1. Creates _get_case_start_time() helper function to handle both schemas
2. Supports backward compatibility with old metadata files (start_time)
3. Supports new metadata files (first_time)
4. Provides clear error messages if neither column is found

This allows step3 to process mixed old/new metadata files on servers that may
have both legacy and newly-generated data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(maglev): re-enable Maglev execution with dead arguments elimination

Re-add replace_dead_arguments() optimizer pass that replaces unused
CreateMapped/UnmappedArguments/CreateRestParameter with UndefinedConstant
in-place. This preserves graph structure (node count, positions) while
eliminating the expensive trampoline call.

Re-enable try_execute_maglev() which was disabled because every sloppy-mode
function triggered unconditional deopt via CreateMappedArguments. With dead
arguments elimination, most functions no longer have this node and can
execute successfully in Maglev.

Expected impact: ~70-800x improvement on all 9 benchmarks (interpreter-only
was 58-5982µs, Maglev was 0.7-3.0µs for the working benchmarks).

Fix PDB Symbol Resolution for Unmerged Windows Traces (#2407)

* Fix PDB symbol resolution for unmerged Windows traces

The ELF symbol work introduced a switch on BinaryFormat in
LookupSymbolsForModule that only handled PE and ELF cases. For
unmerged Windows traces, symbolInfo is null (no RSDS events in the
ETL), so BinaryFormat returns Unspecified, causing symbol resolution
to be skipped entirely.

Add a case for ModuleBinaryFormat.Unspecified that falls back to PDB
lookup on Windows. OpenPdbForModuleFile handles missing PDB signatures
gracefully by checking the local file on disk.

Also rename ModuleBinaryFormat.Unknown to Unspecified to better reflect
that the format was not specified in the trace data.
